source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.

identity.name: Morphous Oak Leaf
identity.motif: deep green lobed oak leaf with tan veins and acorn-brown stem

typography: Geist for crisp planning dashboards with Noto Sans JP guidance for compact Japanese labels, status rows, dates, and calm workflow hierarchy.
layout: Grounded 8px planning grids, leaf-vein dividers, compact dashboard bands, tan table surfaces, restrained shadows, and woodland spacing.

prompts.label: Transparent oak leaf motif
prompts.prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous Oak Leaf transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only one oak leaf botanical motif for a design-system catalog, isolated as a single complete subject on a removable chroma-key background.
Subject: A single lobed oak leaf with botanical accuracy, deep forest-green blade, visible branching veins, subtle tan dry-edge highlights, acorn-brown petiole, grounded oak woodland character, crisp natural texture, no acorn fruit and no extra leaves.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; show the full leaf and stem without cropping, slightly angled but mostly flat for clean silhouette. Clean studio lighting, no cast shadow.
Chroma-key background: perfectly flat solid #ff00ff background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no text, no watermark, no props, no landscape, no branch, no acorn, no second leaf, no decorative background, no shadow, no reflection.

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Oak Leaf light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Oak Leaf", derived from the visible transparent oak leaf cutout. This board is a source reference for implementing a shadcn/tweakcn-compatible grounded planning system.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, 3840 x 2160 composition, clean professional UI design-system presentation.
Required visible sections: Morphous Oak Leaf, Oak Leaf, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Reference use: Extract the palette and interaction language from the oak leaf: deep oak-leaf green, darker vein shadows, tan dry-edge highlights, acorn-brown petiole, woodland tan neutrals, and calm planning accents. The board must read as oak leaf and oak woodland, not generic green UI.
Typography: English product UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; labels such as Geist, Noto Sans JP, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, 日本語 UI. Keep text short, large, and readable.
UI coverage: buttons, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, search command palette, sidebar navigation, dashboard metric cards, planning calendar, burndown or progress chart, empty/loading/error states, token swatches, spacing scale, radius sample set to 8px, border and shadow samples, subtle leaf-vein texture.
Style: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style product design system, precise 8px grid, 8px card radius, warm parchment background, deep green primary actions, acorn-brown secondary controls, tan surfaces, leaf-vein dividers, grounded planning workflow mood, restrained shadows, tactile woodland texture.
Color constraints: use only motif-derived colors and near-neutrals from the oak leaf; no unrelated bright colors, no generic purple/blue gradients, no neon, no saturated tropical colors.
Text constraints: no long paragraphs, no lorem ipsum, no random brand names, no misspelling of "Morphous Oak Leaf", no watermark, no tiny unreadable token tables.

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Oak Leaf dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for the Morphous theme named "Morphous Oak Leaf". It must feel like the visible light-mode board translated to dark mode, not a new theme, and it must remain derived from the visible oak leaf cutout.
Canvas: wide 16:9 board, 3840 x 2160 composition, clean professional UI design-system presentation.
Required visible sections: Morphous Oak Leaf, Oak Leaf, Dark Mode, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Reference use: Preserve the oak leaf system language: deep leaf-shadow backgrounds, pale parchment text, oak green active states, acorn-brown secondary actions, tan woodland surfaces, leaf-vein dividers, subtle dry-edge highlights, 8px shadcn/tweakcn component rhythm, grounded planning workflow mood.
Typography: English product UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; labels such as Geist, Noto Sans JP, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, 日本語 UI. Keep text short, large, and readable.
UI coverage: buttons, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, search command palette, sidebar navigation, dashboard metric cards, planning calendar, burndown or progress chart, empty/loading/error states, token swatches, spacing scale, radius sample set to 8px, border and shadow samples, subtle leaf-vein texture.
Visual details: dark green-black oak canopy background, acorn-brown controls, tan dividers, parchment foreground, restrained contrast, crisp cards and controls, leaf-vein texture overlays, grounded project planning layout.
Color constraints: use only motif-derived colors and near-neutrals from the oak leaf; no neon colors, no unrelated saturated colors, no generic purple/blue gradients, no pure-black-only flat background.
Text constraints: no long paragraphs, no lorem ipsum, no random brand names, no misspelling of "Morphous Oak Leaf", no watermark, no tiny unreadable token tables.
